info.name: URL Shortener (TinyURL clone)
info.description:
All HTTP endpoints exposed by the URL shortener service.

**Quick start**:
1. `Run` the `Shorten URL` request first. It captures `shortCode` into a collection variable.
2. Subsequent requests (Redirect, Get info, Stats) reuse `{{shortCode}}` automatically.

No authentication is required -- this service is anonymous-shortening only.

Default `base_url` is `http://localhost:8090` (the port mapped in `docker-compose.yml`).

Endpoints:
- POST /api/urls                     -- create a short URL
- GET  /{shortCode}                  -- 302 redirect (root path, NOT /api)
- GET  /api/urls/{shortCode}         -- metadata
- GET  /api/urls/{shortCode}/stats   -- aggregated click stats

request.description:
Body fields:
- `longUrl` (required): the full URL to shorten. Must start with `http://` or `https://`.
- `ttlHours` (optional): how long this short URL stays valid. Defaults to 8760 (1 year).

Public redirect. Returns HTTP 302 with the original URL in `Location`. Postman follows redirects by default -- toggle 'Automatically follow redirects' OFF in Settings to inspect the 302 response itself.

Every redirect fires a click event into Kafka (fire-and-forget). Errors in the analytics pipeline never affect this response.

Returns `{ shortCode, longUrl, createdAt, expiresAt, creatorIp }`. Useful for inspecting a code without consuming a click event.

404 if the code doesn't exist.
Demonstrates the Jakarta Validation + ProblemDetail integration. Empty body -> 400 with `application/problem+json` body listing the failing field.
Demonstrates the @Pattern check on `longUrl` -- only http(s) URLs are accepted.
Demonstrates the 404 path on the redirect endpoint.
